verified.name: TheCocktailDB
verified.tagline: Cocktail recipes, ingredients and glassware
verified.description: TheCocktailDB is a crowd-sourced drinks database with the free test key `1` embedded in the URL path. Pull a random cocktail, search by name, or filter by ingredient — every response is JSON with measures, instructions and thumbnail images. Sister project to TheMealDB.

verified.name: D&D 5e API
verified.tagline: Dungeons & Dragons 5e spells, monsters and classes
verified.description: The D&D 5e API serves the System Reference Document as linked JSON: spells, monsters, classes, equipment and rules. No key, permissive CORS, and stable resource URLs — the go-to backend for character builders and homebrew tools.

sampleJson.name: Fireball
desc: A bright streak flashes from your pointing finger to a point you choose within range and then blossoms with a low roar into an explosion of flame. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on that point must make a dexterity saving throw. A target takes 8d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
The fire spreads around corners. It ignites flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 3rd.
sampleJson.material: A tiny ball of bat guano and sulfur.

verified.name: Magic: The Gathering
verified.tagline: Magic: The Gathering cards, sets and types
verified.description: The magicthegathering.io API exposes every MTG card with mana cost, rulings, set and multiverse IDs. Paginated JSON, no authentication, and generous rate limits for hobby projects. Useful for deck builders and card-price companions.

verified.name: iTunes Search
verified.tagline: Search Apple Music, iTunes, App Store and podcasts
verified.description: Apple's public iTunes Search API returns music, apps, movies, books and podcasts as JSON with artwork URLs and preview links. No key required; keep requests modest per Apple's guidance. Widely used for music metadata and podcast directories.

verified.name: Deezer
verified.tagline: Music catalog — tracks, albums, artists and previews
verified.description: Deezer's public API returns track, album and artist metadata with 30-second preview URLs. Search and object-lookup routes need no key; OAuth is only for user playlists. CORS is closed, so call it server-side. Great for music-discovery UIs.

verified.name: Urban Dictionary
verified.tagline: Crowd-sourced slang definitions and examples
verified.description: The unofficial-but-stable Urban Dictionary API returns definitions, usage examples and up/down votes for any term. Keyless JSON; note that content is user-submitted and may be NSFW, so filter before showing to general audiences.

list.author: Nathanmx
list.definition:
API = application programming interface

An API is a series of functions that programs can use to make the operating system do their dirty work. Using [Windows] APIs, for example, a program can open [windows], files, and message boxes--as well as perform more complicated tasks--by passing a single instruction. [Windows] has several classes of APIs that deal with telephony, messaging, and other issues.
list.example: [Windows] uses an api called the Win32 API.  You can [access] many command [via] the command prompt. Start >> Run >> Type in "command" or "cmd"

list.author: Tyler Menezes
list.definition: Adaptive [Pie] Interface. Used by various sites to interact with their [pie] servers. Urban Dictionary, for example, has created APIs so that developers with no [pie] servers of their own my serve pies to customers at low rates.

verified.name: TimeAPI.io
verified.tagline: Current time, time-zone and conversion by zone
verified.description: TimeAPI.io returns the current date-time for any IANA time zone plus conversion, day-of-week and DST helpers. Free and keyless JSON — a modern option for scheduling apps that need reliable time-zone math without bundling tzdata.

verified.name: Binance
verified.tagline: Live crypto spot prices and market data
verified.description: Binance's public market-data endpoints return spot prices, order books and klines without an API key (keys are only for trading). The /ticker/price route is the lightest quote check. Respect the weight-based rate limits in production.

verified.name: Coinbase
verified.tagline: Crypto and fiat exchange rates from Coinbase
verified.description: Coinbase's public v2 endpoints return exchange rates, spot/buy/sell prices and currency lists with no authentication. Pass any base currency to /exchange-rates for a full cross-rate map. Ideal for lightweight crypto tickers and converters.

verified.name: Kraken
verified.tagline: Public crypto ticker, order book and OHLC data
verified.description: Kraken's public REST endpoints expose ticker, depth, spread and OHLC data keyless (auth is trading-only). Responses wrap payloads in an {error, result} envelope. A solid, well-documented source for crypto price feeds.

verified.name: Blockchain.info
verified.tagline: Bitcoin price across dozens of fiat currencies
verified.description: Blockchain.info's /ticker returns the current BTC price — 15-minute delayed plus buy/sell — across 30+ fiat currencies as JSON. Keyless and long-lived; the wider Blockchain Data API also exposes blocks and transactions for on-chain explorers.

verified.name: BigDataCloud Geocode
verified.tagline: Client-side reverse geocoding — no key
verified.description: BigDataCloud's reverse-geocode-client endpoint turns latitude/longitude into locality, city and country without an API key (it is free for client-side use). Returns localized place names and the admin hierarchy — handy for "where am I" features and location labels.

sampleJson.countryName: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland (the)

verified.name: ipwho.is
verified.tagline: IP geolocation with connection and timezone data
verified.description: ipwho.is resolves any IPv4/IPv6 (or the caller IP at the root path) to country, city, coordinates, timezone, currency and ASN. Free, keyless JSON with a `success` flag and generous limits — a drop-in for the many keyed IP-lookup services.

verified.name: Elden Ring API
verified.tagline: Elden Ring items, bosses, weapons and NPCs
verified.description: A community Elden Ring database exposing bosses, weapons, armor, spells, classes and NPCs as paginated JSON. No key; responses carry a count/total envelope for easy paging. Popular for build planners and wiki mirrors.

data.name: Hero
data.description: A stalwart Hero, at home with a battleaxe, descended from a badlands chieftain

verified.name: SampleAPIs
verified.tagline: Ready-made mock datasets for prototyping
verified.description: SampleAPIs serves themed fake datasets — coffee, cocktails, movies, cars, football and more — as static JSON arrays. No key, CORS-open, and perfect for wiring up a frontend before the real backend exists.

sampleJson.title: Latte
sampleJson.description: Som den mest populära kaffedrycken där ute består latte av en skvätt espresso och ångad mjölk med bara en gnutta skum. Den kan beställas utan smak eller med smak av allt från vanilj till pumpa kryddor.

sampleJson.title: Caramel Latte
sampleJson.description: Om du gillar latte med en speciell smak kan karamell latte vara det bästa alternativet för att ge dig en upplevelse av den naturliga sötman och krämigheten hos ångad mjölk och karamell.

verified.name: Affirmations.dev
verified.tagline: Random positive affirmation as tiny JSON
verified.description: affirmations.dev returns a single uplifting affirmation per request in a one-field JSON body. No key, CORS-open, and about as simple as an API gets — a friendly hello-world fetch or a daily-motivation widget.

sampleJson.affirmation: Nothing is impossible. The word itself says 'I’m possible!'

verified.name: FBI Wanted
verified.tagline: FBI Most Wanted list as structured JSON
verified.description: The FBI's public Wanted API returns current cases — fugitives, missing persons and seeking-information notices — with photos, descriptions, subjects and field offices. No key; paginate with page/pageSize. Official US government open data.

items.reward_text: The FBI is offering a reward of up to $25,000 for information leading to the arrest and conviction of the individual(s) responsible for the death of Patrick Carlow, Sr.

verified.name: TheAudioDB
verified.tagline: Artist, album and track metadata with artwork
verified.description: TheAudioDB is a community music-metadata database with the free test key `2` in the path. Search artists, list albums and tracks, and pull banners, logos and biographies. Sister site to TheSportsDB and TheMealDB.

verified.name: ExchangeRate-API (open)
verified.tagline: Daily fiat exchange rates — open, no key
verified.description: The open ExchangeRate-API endpoint returns daily-updated fiat rates for 160+ currencies with no key or signup. One GET on /latest/{base} yields the full cross-rate table plus next-update timestamps. Good for converters that can tolerate daily granularity.

verified.name: wger Workout
verified.tagline: Open exercise and nutrition database
verified.description: wger is an open-source workout manager whose REST API exposes exercises, muscles, equipment and ingredients. Read endpoints are public and keyless (append format=json); a token is only needed to write to a user account. Great for fitness-app prototypes.

verified.name: Zenodo
verified.tagline: Search open research datasets and publications
verified.description: Zenodo (run by CERN) hosts open datasets, papers and software with DOIs. Its API searches and fetches records with metadata, files and citation info — keyless for read access, though a token raises rate limits. A front door to open-science outputs.

metadata.title: Mendelian Randomization Predicts Drug-Target Success Only for Abundance-Modulating Mechanisms: A Pre-Registered, Outcome-Blind Evaluation
metadata.description:
Mendelian randomization (MR) using cis-protein quantitative trait loci (pQTLs) is widely used to prioritize drug targets, yet its predictive validity has never been evaluated with respect to the mechanism by which a drug acts on its target. We present a pre-registered, outcome-blind evaluation of a frozen, zero-parameter cis-pQTL MR classifier against 195 Phase III drug-target pairs across 24 diseases, drawn from UKB-PPP and EpiGraphDB instruments matched to Open Targets. Of these, 138 pairs (71%) had evaluable MR results; 57 were missing due to instrument-GWAS panel incompatibility.

Our primary finding is a pre-registered mechanism dissociation: MR is uninformative for activity-blocking drug targets (balanced accuracy [BA] = 0.511, 90% CI [0.456, 0.576]; n = 76), confirming a priori that a genetic instrument for protein abundance cannot detect drugs that inhibit protein function without changing protein level. For abundance-modulating targets, MR trends positive (BA = 0.590, 90% CI [0.496, 0.691]; n = 59) but the confidence interval includes chance, and missingness disproportionately depletes successes from the analyzed set. A continuous ROC analysis provides the strongest pooled evidence of discrimination (AUC = 0.595, permutation p = 0.038). Leave-one-disease-out analysis confirms no single disease drives the result (BA range [0.538, 0.584]). The pooled BA of 0.559 ([0.503, 0.617]) barely clears chance and drops to 0.533 under worst-plausible missingness assumptions.

The contribution is the dissociation itself: MR-based target validation has a definable domain of validity set by drug mechanism, and the boundary between informative and uninformative strata is a structural property of the genetic instrument. Within the abundance domain, a significant MR result is a positive prioritization signal (PPV = 0.56); outside it, MR is silent by construction.

verified.name: GBIF
verified.tagline: Global biodiversity — species and occurrences
verified.description: The Global Biodiversity Information Facility API serves taxonomic names, species records and billions of occurrence points contributed by museums and researchers worldwide. Keyless JSON with rich filtering — the standard source for biodiversity and mapping tools.

verified.name: iNaturalist
verified.tagline: Species, observations and photos from iNaturalist
verified.description: iNaturalist's API exposes crowd-sourced wildlife observations, taxa and identification photos. Read endpoints are public and keyless (OAuth only for posting). Filter by taxon, place or user — ideal for nature-ID apps and citizen-science dashboards.

verified.name: Platzi Fake Store
verified.tagline: Fake e-commerce API — products, users, auth
verified.description: The Platzi Fake Store API provides realistic e-commerce data — products, categories, users and JWT auth — with full CRUD for practice. Keyless and CORS-open; changes reset periodically. A step up from read-only mock APIs for building shop demos.

sampleJson.title: Classic White Tee - Timeless Style and Comfort
sampleJson.description: Elevate your everyday wardrobe with our Classic White Tee. Crafted from premium soft cotton material, this versatile t-shirt combines comfort with durability, perfect for daily wear. Featuring a relaxed, unisex fit that flatters every body type, it's a staple piece for any casual ensemble. Easy to care for and machine washable, this white tee retains its shape and softness wash after wash. Pair it with your favorite jeans or layer it under a jacket for a smart look.

verified.name: restful-api.dev
verified.tagline: Free CRUD sandbox for objects — no signup
verified.description: restful-api.dev is a free hosted REST sandbox: GET a list of sample objects and POST/PUT/DELETE your own for testing HTTP clients. No key or signup, JSON throughout. Handy for tutorials, Postman demos and integration tests.

verified.name: Lichess
verified.tagline: Open chess data — players, games and puzzles
verified.description: Lichess exposes profiles, ratings, game exports (PGN/JSON) and tournaments through a public API. Many read endpoints need no token (one raises rate limits and unlocks account routes). The go-to open alternative to Chess.com for chess tooling.

skipped.reason: probe failed HTTP err TypeError: fetch failed
